M05-1-title.title: M05 AI Pipeline Workshop
M05-1-title.subtitle: Drive AI Generation with Bone-Meat-Soul
M05-1-title.notes: This morning is the session trainees look forward to most — using the framework to drive AI. Open by building anticipation.

M05-1-review.title: Bone-Meat-Soul Three-Layer Review
M05-1-review.studentContent:
🦴 Bone Layer

HOW to tell

Skeleton types
Strategy L0/L1/L2 three-level system

"How to structure the story"

👻 Soul Layer

WHY it works

Target audience, trigger scenario
Reward type, conflict type, emotion arc

"Why it moves people"

🥩 Meat Layer

HOW it looks & sounds

Visuals, audio, pacing
Subtitles, effects, duration

"How to present it"

Today's focus: Just the Bone layer knowledge alone — Section order + Strategy annotations — is already enough to drive AI to generate high-quality script frameworks!
M05-1-review.presenterContent: 💡 Teaching Tip: Quick review to help trainees recall yesterday's content. You can ask: "Who can tell me in one sentence — what question does each layer answer?"
M05-1-review.notes: Quick review of the three BMS layers. Guide trainees to answer, then supplement. Today's focus: using the Bone layer to drive AI.

M05-1-core.title: Core Concept: AI Is a Tool, Not Magic
M05-1-core.studentContent:
AI Needs Humans to Direct It

✅ The Value of Framework

  • Bone layer structural choices and Strategy configuration → Human decision
  • Soul layer audience definition and conflict design → Human decision
  • Meat layer specific content generation → AI accelerates

The framework gives you judgment — you can tell where AI got it right and where it got it wrong

❌ Common Mistakes

Wrong approach

Directly asking AI to "write me a product video script"

Right approach

First use the BMS framework to determine Skeleton, Strategy, and Soul Profile, then let AI generate based on structured input

⚠️ Without a framework, you can't even judge whether AI's output is good or bad!
M05-1-core.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
Many people think that with AI, you don't need to think at all. That's wrong.

AI is a powerful tool, but it needs humans to direct it. The framework gives you judgment — you can tell where AI got it right and where it got it wrong. Without a framework, you can't even judge whether AI's output is good or bad.
M05-1-core.notes: Correct the misconception that 'AI can do everything.' Emphasize the framework as the core capability; AI is just an accelerator.

M05-2-title.title: AI Pipeline Workflow
M05-2-title.subtitle: Structured Input → Structured Output
M05-2-title.notes: Introduce the complete AI-assisted content production workflow.

M05-2-concept.title: Core Idea: Structured Input, Structured Output
M05-2-concept.studentContent:
Structured Input → AI Processing → Structured Output

❌ Vague Input

"Write me an ad"

→ AI doesn't know what kind of ad you want
→ Output is generic and unusable

✅ Structured Input

"I want a video using the HBrPBVC structure, with the HOOK using HAND/SUSPENSE/Question strategy, targeting 25-35 year-old night owls..."

→ AI knows your expectations
→ Output is precise and actionable

AI is not a mind reader — your input quality directly determines AI's output quality
M05-2-concept.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
Why do we need structured input? Because AI is not a mind reader. If you just give AI a single line like "write me an ad," it has no idea what kind of ad you want. But if you tell AI the detailed structure and strategies, AI knows exactly what you expect.
M05-2-concept.notes: Use comparison to illustrate the importance of structured input.

M05-2-pipeline.title: Complete 7-Step AI Pipeline
M05-2-pipeline.studentContent:
StepContentDescriptionWho does it
Step 1Choose SkeletonDetermine skeleton type (e.g., HPBVC 5-section, HBrPBVC 6-section)Human
Step 2Configure Strategy per SectionAssign L0/L1/L2 three-level strategy to each SectionHuman
Step 3Configure Soul ProfileTarget audience, trigger scenario, conflict type, reward type, emotion arcHuman
Step 4Configure Meat ProfileAudio-visual style requirements — visual tone, BGM style, pacing controlHuman
Step 5Enter Product InfoProduct name, core selling points, price range, promotionsHuman
Step 6AI GenerationBased on 5 layers of structured input, AI generates the script frameworkAI
Step 7Human Review/EditUse the Strategy system to check each section, correct annotation errorsHuman
💡 Key Insight: Out of 7 steps, the first 5 are all done by humans. AI only handles Step 6 (generation), and Step 7 goes back to human review.
AI is the draft machine, humans are the quality controllers.
M05-2-pipeline.presenterContent: 💡 Teaching Focus: Emphasize that 6 out of 7 steps are human work. Input quality directly determines output quality — garbage in, garbage out.
M05-2-pipeline.notes: Complete 7-step workflow. Emphasize AI only handles Step 6; all others are human work.

M05-2-formula.title: Pipeline Formula & Efficiency Gains
M05-2-formula.studentContent:
Template (Skeleton + Strategy) + SoulProfile + MeatProfile + Product Info
→ AI Generation → Script Output → Human Review/Edit

📊 Efficiency Gains Reference

Script writing3-5x faster
Creative ideation10+ variations at once
Multi-language adaptationInstant translation
Batch production10+ scripts per day

🎯 Today's Focus

The full Pipeline involves three layers of input, but today we'll focus on the most essential part —

Using only the Bone layer (Section + Strategy) to drive AI

This layer alone can already produce high-quality output!
M05-2-formula.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
Look at this formula — the full Pipeline has three layers of input: Bone layer, Soul layer, and Meat layer. But today we're focusing only on the Bone layer. Why? Because Section + Strategy alone can already drive AI to produce high-quality scripts.

Take a look at the efficiency data: script writing 3-5x faster, 10+ variations generated at once. This isn't replacing you — it's fitting you with a turbocharger.
M05-2-formula.notes: Show the Pipeline formula and efficiency data. Emphasize today's focus is Bone layer only.

M05-3-title.title: Prompt Engineering Workshop
M05-3-title.subtitle: Deconstructing the AI Prompt Segment by Segment
M05-3-title.notes: Prompt segment-by-segment walkthrough. Each segment maps to specific BMS framework knowledge.

M05-3-principles.title: Three Principles of Prompt Engineering
M05-3-principles.studentContent:
1️⃣ Structured Input

Use clear frameworks and parameters to tell AI exactly what you want

How it shows in the Prompt

Section order + Strategy L0/L1/L2 annotation scope

2️⃣ Formatted Output

Strictly define AI's output format so results are controllable

How it shows in the Prompt

Table format + three columns per row (Section / Strategy / One-liner)

3️⃣ Negative Constraints

Explicitly tell AI what NOT to do to prevent going off-track

How it shows in the Prompt

"Don't write psychological explanations" "Don't explain structure" "Must be distinctly different" "Must not invent new labels"

Structured Input + Formatted Output + Negative Constraints = High-Quality AI Output
M05-3-principles.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
The core principles of good prompt writing aren't as simple as "just tell AI what's in your head." Remember these three: Structured Input, Formatted Output, Negative Constraints. This is the core of Prompt Engineering.
M05-3-principles.notes: The three PE principles form the theoretical foundation for the upcoming Prompt deconstruction.

M05-3-p1.title: Prompt Segment 1: Role Definition
M05-3-p1.studentContent:
🎯 Role Definition

You are a short-video structure designer, specialized in producing
camera-ready script frameworks within a given Section and Strategy framework.
Based on the specified Section order + Strategy system, generate:
10 directly executable script structure frameworks
💡 Why this role definition?
  • The role is "structure designer," not copywriter, not creative director
  • The role definition determines the type of AI output — we want structured script frameworks, not prose
  • Clear output quantity: 10 sets, ensuring diversity
M05-3-p1.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
Segment one — Role Definition. We tell AI it's a "short-video structure designer," not a copywriter. The role definition determines the type of AI output.
M05-3-p1.notes: Role definition determines AI's output direction. Emphasize 'structure designer' rather than 'copywriter.'

M05-3-p2.title: Prompt Segment 2: Product Scenario
M05-3-p2.studentContent:
🎯 Product Scenario

Product: Whitening supplement (oral beauty supplement)
Objective: Seeding + Conversion
Audience: Women interested in brightening skin tone, fighting dullness, improving complexion

👻 Soul Layer Info (Simplified)

This is a simplified Soul Profile — who is the video for, what problem to solve, what objective to achieve

📋 Full Soul Profile: 5 Fields

• target_audience
• trigger_scenario
• reward_type
• conflict_type
• emotion_arc

In production use, fill in all 5 fields for more precise AI output
M05-3-p2.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
Segment two — Product Scenario. This is the simplified version of Soul layer info. Today's demo uses the simplified version; in production, you can fill in all 5 fields.
M05-3-p2.notes: Simplified Soul layer. Mention the full 5 fields but use simplified version today.

M05-3-p3.title: Prompt Segment 3: Fixed Section Structure
M05-3-p3.studentContent:
🎯 Fixed Section Structure (Skeleton)

Must use:
HOOK → BRIDGE → PROBLEM → RESOLUTION → VALUE → CTA
REVERSAL is optional.
🦴 This is the Bone layer's Skeleton!

Using HBrPBVC — 6-section full setup type (Tier C level)

  • Most sections, most complete narrative
  • Best suited for in-depth seeding + conversion content
  • REVERSAL (plot twist) is optional, placed between PROBLEM and RESOLUTION

The SSOT contains 8 valid Skeletons — from Tier A basic 4-section to Tier C with BRIDGE structure
M05-3-p3.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
This is the Skeleton we learned yesterday! Here we're using HBrPBVC — the 6-section full setup type. We chose it because it's the most complete, showcasing the most Section and Strategy combinations.
M05-3-p3.notes: HBrPBVC 6-section skeleton. Can quickly review the 8 valid skeletons.

M05-3-p4.title: Prompt Segment 4: Strategy System Annotation ⭐
M05-3-p4.studentContent:
🎯 Strategy System Annotation (Most Critical!)

Each Section must be annotated with L0 / L1 / L2 three-level strategy

HEAD Cognition & Trust

├── EXPLAIN → Logic / Data / Transparency
├── PROOF → UGC / Before_After / Sales_Data
├── AUTHORITY → Expert / Certification / Media / Guarantee
└── CONTRAST → AB / Shocking / Value_Stack

HEART Emotion & Empathy

├── PAIN → Scene / Daily / Loss
├── FEAR → Consequence / Miss_Out
└── STORY → Origin / User

EYES Visual & Experience

└── DEMO → Step / Multi_Angle

HAND Attraction & Action

├── SUSPENSE → Question / Reveal
└── URGENCY → Time / Stock
⚠️ Must choose ONLY from the list above. Must NOT invent new L1 or L2 names.
4 L0s · 10 L1s · 26 L2s — This is how the Strategy system works in practice
M05-3-p4.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script (Critical!)
This segment is the most critical part of the entire Prompt! We wrote the complete L0→L1→L2 tree structure into the Prompt, and explicitly told AI — "You must choose only from this list, and must not invent new labels."

Why? Because without this constraint, AI will invent its own labels! It will write things like CURIOSITY, RELEVANCE — labels that don't exist in our system.
M05-3-p4.notes: This is the most critical part of the entire Prompt! Complete Strategy tree + negative constraints to prevent AI from inventing labels.

M05-3-p5.title: Prompt Segment 5: Output Format Requirements
M05-3-p5.studentContent:
❗ Output Requirements (Strict Format)

Each framework must be output in table format:

Framework X: Name this structure (e.g., Authority Expert Type / Anxiety Alert Type / Cognitive Disruption Type)
Skeleton: HOOK → BRIDGE → PROBLEM → RESOLUTION → VALUE → CTA
SectionStrategy (L0/L1/L2)One-liner (camera-ready)
HOOKXXX / XXX / XXX"A line you can film directly"
BRIDGEXXX / XXX / XXX"A line you can film directly"
PROBLEMXXX / XXX / XXX"A line you can film directly"
RESOLUTIONXXX / XXX / XXX"A line you can film directly"
VALUEXXX / XXX / XXX"A line you can film directly"
CTAXXX / XXX / XXX"A line you can film directly"
💡 Embodiment of the "Formatted Output" principle: To make AI output controllable, the format must be strictly defined. Three columns aligned, structure clear.
M05-3-p5.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
Segment five is the output format requirements. Notice the three aligned columns: Section / Strategy / One-liner.

This is the concrete embodiment of our three principles' "Formatted Output." If you don't specify the format, AI will write you an essay — define a table format, and the output becomes controllable, reviewable, and reusable at scale.
M05-3-p5.notes: Table format with three aligned columns. This is the concrete embodiment of the 'Formatted Output' principle.

M05-3-p6.title: Prompt Segment 6: Key Constraints (Negative Constraints)
M05-3-p6.studentContent:
❗ Key Constraints (4 Negative Constraints)

1️⃣ "Camera-Ready"

Don't write psychological explanations. Don't write structural descriptions. Write dialogue or on-camera lines directly.

❌ Wrong

"Establish authority"

✅ Correct

"A dermatologist says: 99% of people are whitening the wrong way"

2️⃣ Each framework must be distinctly different

E.g., Authority Expert Type / Anxiety Amplifier Type / Cognitive Disruption Type / Identity-Driven Type... Can't just swap words.

3️⃣ Don't explain the structure

Don't explain why it's Problem or why it's Resolution. Just give the table results.

4️⃣ Don't write a full script

Only provide structure + one-liner. Output quantity: generate 10 complete frameworks.
M05-3-p6.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
These constraints were refined through multiple iterations. Pay special attention to #1 — "Camera-Ready." Without this constraint, AI will output a bunch of useless phrases like "establish authority here." What we want are lines you can say directly to the camera.
M05-3-p6.notes: Negative constraints are lessons refined through multiple iterations. Especially emphasize the importance of 'Camera-Ready.'

M05-3-mapping.title: BMS Knowledge Mapping + Live Demo
M05-3-mapping.studentContent:
Prompt ModuleCorresponding BMS KnowledgeFrom Which Layer
Section fixed structureHBrPBVC 6-section skeletonBone — Skeleton
Strategy annotation requirementsL0/L1/L2 three-level strategy + constraint listBone — Strategy
Product / Audience / Objectivetarget_audience + reward_typeSoul (simplified)
Output format constraintsSection × Strategy tableBone
"Camera-ready" constraintEnsures output is close to executable standardMeat
💡 Core Insight: A good Prompt is essentially "translating" the framework knowledge in your head into structured instructions that AI can understand.
Clearer framework → Better Prompt → More accurate AI output
🖥️ Live Demo: Paste the complete Prompt into an AI tool (ChatGPT / Claude both work), run it live, and observe the output.
M05-3-mapping.presenterContent: 💡 Action Tip: While waiting for AI to generate, chat and interact with trainees. If the AI tool has issues, use the 10 backup frameworks from the next section for the analysis.
M05-3-mapping.notes: The BMS knowledge mapping table helps trainees understand the correspondence between the Prompt and the framework. Then run the Prompt live.

M05-4-title.title: Output Analysis: 10 Framework Breakdown
M05-4-title.subtitle: Three Rounds of Deep AI Output Analysis
M05-4-title.notes: Three rounds of analysis after AI output: feel the impact → Strategy error-checking → cross-framework comparison. This is a key segment.

M05-4-fw12.title: Framework 1: Cognitive Disruption + Framework 2: Social Anxiety
M05-4-fw12.studentContent:
Framework 1: Cognitive Disruption

SectionStrategyOne-liner
HOOKHAND/SUSPENSE/Question"Stop just using sunscreen — the sun isn't what's making your face yellow!"
BRIDGEHEART/PAIN/Scene"Why does your face still look rusty even after layering on sunscreen?"
PROBLEMHEART/FEAR/Consequence"You're being 'glycated' — slowly caramelizing like grilled meat"
REVERSALHEAD/CONTRAST/Shocking"It's actually 'sugar face' — sunscreen can't block the boba tea you're drinking"
VALUEHEAD/EXPLAIN/Transparency"Anti-glycation patented ingredient — an internal scavenger targeting stubborn yellowness"
CTAHAND/URGENCY/Time"Two pills a day, get your glow back"

Path: Disrupt cognition → Pain empathy → Reattribute cause → New solution → Action

Framework 2: Social Anxiety

SectionStrategyOne-liner
HOOKHEAD/CONTRAST/Shocking"The most awkward person in group photos? The one who looks like they didn't wash their face"
BRIDGEHEART/PAIN/Scene"Filters only fix your social media, not the awkward meeting with your client"
PROBLEMHEART/PAIN/Daily"Dull skin makes you look 'worn out' — no designer outfit can save that"
RESOLUTIONHEAD/EXPLAIN/Logic"You're not dark — you just haven't lit up yet"
VALUEHEAD/PROOF/Before_After"This is an eaten-in natural glow — bare face brighter than makeup"
CTAHAND/URGENCY/Time"Want to be the star in every group photo? Tap the link below"

Path: Visual pain point → Scene sting → Ideal identity → Effect comparison → Desire-driven
M05-4-fw12.presenterContent:
💡 Round 1: Feel the Impact (3 minutes)
Have trainees quickly scan all frameworks and silently pick the "most compelling" one — they'll vote by raising hands later. Purpose: build the belief that "AI can actually deliver."
M05-4-fw12.notes: Show the first two frameworks. Round 1 analysis: let trainees feel AI's capability ceiling.

M05-4-fw34.title: Framework 3: Ingredient Myth-buster + Framework 4: Deep Detox
M05-4-fw34.studentContent:
Framework 3: Ingredient Myth-buster

SectionStrategyOne-liner
HOOKHEAD/CONTRAST/Shocking"Stop bingeing on lemons — you'll wreck your stomach before your face gets any brighter"
BRIDGEHEART/PAIN/Scene"You think vitamin C supplements will whiten your skin? Dead wrong"
PROBLEMHEAD/EXPLAIN/Data"90% of regular vitamin C gets flushed out — you're producing expensive urine"
REVERSALHEAD/CONTRAST/Shocking"Whitening isn't about who takes more — it's about who absorbs more"
VALUEHEAD/PROOF/Sales_Data"18x absorption rate vs. regular vitamin C. One pill does the work of a handful"
CTAHAND/URGENCY/Time"Stop wasting money — choose one that actually absorbs"

Path: Bust the myth → Expose inefficiency → Technical solution → Data proof → Rational decision

Framework 4: Deep Detox

SectionStrategyOne-liner
HOOKHEART/FEAR/Consequence"Spots on your face? That's your body screaming for help"
BRIDGEHEART/PAIN/Daily"Greenish complexion, spots clustered on cheekbones — do you stay up late a lot?"
PROBLEMHEART/FEAR/Consequence"Toxins piling up inside — all the waste is surfacing on your face"
RESOLUTIONHEAD/EXPLAIN/Logic"Clean up your body first — melanin will flush out naturally"
VALUEHEAD/EXPLAIN/Transparency"High-purity glutathione — your liver's favorite 'cleaner'"
CTAHAND/URGENCY/Time"Tackle dullness at the root — start detoxing today"

Path: Health warning → Symptom check → Blame internal organs → Internal care logic → Ingredient support
M05-4-fw34.presenterContent: 💡 Teaching Focus: Framework 3 and Framework 4 form a sharp contrast — Framework 3 takes a full HEAD rational route (ingredient data + myth-busting), while Framework 4 takes a full HEART emotional route (health fear + internal care). Show trainees: the same product can take completely different persuasion paths through rational vs. emotional approaches.
M05-4-fw34.notes: Framework 3 leads with HEAD rational approach; Framework 4 leads with HEART emotional approach.

M05-4-fw56.title: Framework 5: Lazy Efficiency + Framework 6: Time Irreversibility
M05-4-fw56.studentContent:
Framework 5: Lazy Efficiency

SectionStrategyOne-liner
HOOKHEART/PAIN/Scene"Layering morning C and evening A like overtime shifts — still not brighter"
BRIDGEHEART/PAIN/Daily"The hardest part of skincare is sticking with it. On-and-off means zero results"
PROBLEMHEART/PAIN/Loss"Skip one day, three days of serum wasted. How long can you keep this up?"
RESOLUTIONHEAD/EXPLAIN/Logic"Make whitening as easy as drinking water — swallow it and it's working"
VALUEHEAD/CONTRAST/AB"One pill = one whole bottle of serum, and your whole body brightens"
CTAHAND/URGENCY/Time"The lazy person's full-body whitening method — tap here"

Path: Empathize with fatigue → Amplify sunk cost → Easy path → Efficiency comparison

Framework 6: Time Irreversibility

SectionStrategyOne-liner
HOOKEYES/DEMO/Multi_Angle"This is the truth 5mm under your skin"
BRIDGEHEART/PAIN/Daily"Notice a couple new spots recently? That's just the tip of the iceberg"
PROBLEMHEART/FEAR/Consequence"'Invisible spots' are queuing up to surface — laser costs thousands and can still cause rebound"
RESOLUTIONHEAD/EXPLAIN/Logic"Catch them before they surface — crush them at the base layer"
VALUEHEAD/EXPLAIN/Transparency"Exclusive melanin-blocking factor — stop spots before they form"
CTAHAND/URGENCY/Time"Don't wait until your face is covered — intervene now"

Path: Tech reveal → Amplify consequence → Only window → Blocking mechanism → Urgent action
M05-4-fw56.presenterContent: 💡 Teaching Focus: Framework 5's highlight is using "lazy person empathy" as the entry point — first amplify the pain of sticking with a routine, then offer an easy path. Framework 6's highlight is the HOOK using EYES/DEMO to open ("the truth 5mm under your skin") — this is the only one out of 10 frameworks that uses visual impact for the HOOK. Draw trainees' attention to AI's creative diversity.
M05-4-fw56.notes: Framework 5 leads with efficiency comparison; Framework 6's EYES/DEMO opening is a highlight.

M05-4-check.title: Strategy Annotation Check Exercise ⭐
M05-4-check.studentContent:
🎯 Round 2: Strategy Annotation Check (Key Exercise)

AI's Strategy annotations aren't always correct — use the Strategy system you learned yesterday to check!

FrameworkSectionInstructor QuestionTeaching Point
Framework 5VALUE"AI used CONTRAST/AB — do you think that's correct?"✅ Comparing pill vs. serum efficiency = CONTRAST/AB is correct
Framework 9VALUE"What is Value_Stack? Remember?"✅ Price anchoring, bundle combo = Value_Stack is correct
Framework 6HOOK"UV camera showing spots under the skin — what Strategy?"✅ Visual demonstration = EYES/DEMO is correct
Framework 8BRIDGE"72-hour lifesaver — what Strategy?"✅ Time pressure = HAND/URGENCY is correct
💡 Key Insight: Every line of AI output has L0/L1/L2 annotations, and we can use the Strategy system to "judge" AI's output.

This is the value of the framework — it doesn't just help you generate content, it helps you evaluate content.
M05-4-check.presenterContent: 💡 Teaching Method: Pick 3-4 typical cases for trainees to judge. If AI's annotation is wrong, it's the perfect opportunity to demonstrate the value of "human error-correction."
M05-4-check.notes: Round 2 analysis core: using the Strategy system to evaluate AI output. This proves the framework's evaluative value.

M05-4-compare.title: Cross-Framework Comparison
M05-4-compare.studentContent:
🎯 Round 3: Persuasion Path Comparison Across 10 Frameworks

CategoryFramework #Characteristics
HEAD-dominant (Rational)3, 9, 10Ingredients / Data / Authority / Value-for-money
HEART-dominant (Emotional)2, 4, 7Social anxiety / Health fear / Confidence desire
HEAD + HEART Mixed1, 5, 6, 8Cognitive disruption + Emotion / Efficiency + Pain
Includes REVERSAL1, 3Rewriting the mental model
Straight-line progression2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9No REVERSAL
💡 Same product, 10 completely different paths.

This is the power of the Bone-Meat-Soul framework — it doesn't give you one fixed formula, but a combinatorial system you can mix and match.

And because the Prompt used a complete Strategy annotation constraint, AI didn't invent any strange labels — every L0/L1/L2 in each line can be found in our system tree. This is the power of "Negative Constraints."
M05-4-compare.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
This table is the summary of the entire Part 4. Same product, same Prompt — AI produced 10 completely different persuasion paths.

This is the core value of the Bone-Meat-Soul framework: it's not a fixed template, but a combinatorial system. Different combinations of Section x Strategy create different persuasion paths.

Also notice: because the Prompt used negative constraints, AI didn't invent any strange labels — every line can be traced back to our system tree.
M05-4-compare.notes: Round 3 analysis: cross-framework comparison. Emphasize 'combinatorial system' rather than 'fixed formula.'

M05-5-title.title: Hands-on Practice
M05-5-title.subtitle: Modify Variables, Run the Prompt, Evaluate Output
M05-5-title.notes: Group hands-on practice. Each group modifies key parameters in the Prompt template.

M05-5-steps.title: 5 Practice Steps + 3 Variables to Modify
M05-5-steps.studentContent:
📋 5 Practice Steps

StepTimeContent
15minReceive product info card, determine target audience
25minModify 3 variables in the Prompt
35minRun the modified Prompt
415minEvaluate output: check each section using Strategy system
510minEach group shares best framework + instructor feedback

🔧 3 Variables to Modify

VariableOriginal PromptChange to
ProductWhitening supplementProduct assigned to your group
ObjectiveSeeding + ConversionAdjust based on product
AudienceWomen interested in brighteningAdjust based on target audience
💡 Skeleton structure and output format stay unchanged.
First experience the effect of "only changing variables, not the structure."
M05-5-steps.presenterContent: ▶ Instructor Action: Distribute product info cards, roam and guide, assist with AI tool operation.
M05-5-steps.notes: Hands-on core: only change 3 variables (product/objective/audience), skeleton and format stay the same.

M05-5-checklist.title: Evaluation Checklist + Common Mistakes
M05-5-checklist.studentContent:
✅ Evaluation Checklist (6 Items)

1️⃣ Is the HOOK's Strategy reasonable?
Check compatibility: CONTRAST/PAIN/SUSPENSE recommended
2️⃣ Does the PROBLEM actually "create tension"?
Read the one-liner — does it feel impactful?
3️⃣ Are RESOLUTION and VALUE confused?
RESOLUTION = "There's hope", VALUE = "It's worth buying"
4️⃣ Does the CTA have a clear action instruction?
5️⃣ Are the 10 frameworks different enough?
Compare persuasion paths — did they use different L0 combinations?
6️⃣ Are Strategy annotations within the valid list?
Cross-check with the full L0→L1→L2 tree, look for AI-invented labels

❌ Common Mistakes Warning

Wrong approach

Giving AI a vague instruction — "Write me a beauty video script"

Right approach

Giving AI detailed structured input — which skeleton, what Strategy per section, target audience profile, product selling points

AI's output quality depends on the quality of your input. Garbage in, garbage out.
M05-5-checklist.presenterContent: 💡 Key Guidance: Have trainees use the Strategy system to check whether each section's L0/L1/L2 annotations are reasonable. If they think something is mislabeled, note it for sharing later.
M05-5-checklist.notes: The 6-item checklist is the core tool for hands-on practice.

M05-5-quality.title: AI Output Quality Control + Advanced Challenge
M05-5-quality.studentContent:
⚠️ 5 Common AI Output Issues

1. Strategy annotation drift
AI may use labels not in the system, or annotations that don't match the content
2. Not conversational enough
Reads like an essay, not like spoken language
3. Selling point overload
Tries to say everything, lacks focus
4. Flat emotional tone
No emotional arc, fails to move people
5. Factual errors
AI may fabricate non-existent effects or data
AI is the draft machine, humans are the quality controllers.

🚀 Advanced Challenge

Groups that finish early can try:

Change the Skeleton from 6-section HBrPBVC to 5-section HPBVC (remove BRIDGE)

Observe what changes:

  • Without BRIDGE setup, does the AI's HOOK become more direct?
  • Does the PROBLEM section come faster?
  • How does the overall pacing change?
M05-5-quality.presenterContent:
📢 Instructor Script
After AI generates the output, you must review and edit it manually. Use our Strategy system to check each section, and correct what needs correcting.
M05-5-quality.notes: 5 quality control issues + Advanced challenge (change skeleton and observe differences).

M05-6-title.title: Sharing & Summary
M05-6-title.subtitle: Three Key Takeaways + AI Tool Recommendations
M05-6-title.notes: Summary session. Each group shares for 2 minutes.

M05-6-takeaway.title: Three Key Takeaways + AI Tool Recommendations
M05-6-takeaway.studentContent:
🎯 Three Key Takeaways

1. Power of Structured Input

Give AI a framework and it delivers structured results. The three Prompt principles — Structured Input, Formatted Output, Negative Constraints — are the core methodology for driving AI.

2. The Framework Gives You Judgment

You can tell where AI got it right and where it got it wrong. The Strategy correction exercise proved this — with the L0/L1/L2 system, you have evaluation criteria.

3. AI Is the Draft Machine, Humans Are the QC

Final quality is still the human's responsibility. Out of 7 steps in the Pipeline, 5 are human work.

📝 Copywriting & Scripts

ChatGPTMost versatile — scripts/creative/translation
ClaudeStrong on long-form, complex scripts
Wenxin YiyanGreat Chinese comprehension, localized content

🎬 Images & Video

MidjourneyAI image generation, product scene shots
CapCutVideo editing + AI, all-in-one
KlingAI video generation, creative assets
Even without AI, this framework itself is the most valuable asset. AI is just an accelerator; the framework is the core capability.
M05-6-takeaway.presenterContent:
📢 Sharing Session Guide
Each group shares for 2 minutes:
1. How they configured it (which variables they changed)
2. How the AI-generated result turned out (pick the best framework)
3. What they found needed adjustment (where AI mislabeled)
M05-6-takeaway.notes: Three key takeaways + AI tool recommendations. Emphasize the framework is the core capability; AI is the accelerator.

M05-6-quiz.title: Knowledge Checkpoint
M05-6-quiz.question: In the AI Pipeline workflow, which step must be done by humans — AI can't help?
options: B. Determining Skeleton and Strategy configuration
M05-6-quiz.explanation: Skeleton (structural choice) and Strategy (strategy configuration) must be determined by humans. This involves structural design and persuasion path planning, which AI cannot judge. Similarly, the Soul Profile's audience definition and conflict design must also be done by humans. Out of the 7 steps in the Pipeline, the first 5 are all human work — AI only handles Step 6 (generation).
M05-6-quiz.notes: Which Pipeline step must be human? Answer is B — Skeleton and Strategy configuration. This is the module's core argument.
